steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: On the left: “This designation is hypothetical” is an independent clause (can stand alone). On the right: “Proto-Nilo-Saharan is only postulated, its characteristics being inferred...” is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the conjunctive adverb rule
steps.content: When a conjunctive adverb like however connects two independent clauses, it must either follow a semicolon in one sentence (IC; however, IC) or start a new sentence after a period (IC. However, IC). Choice C follows the second pattern: period before “However,” with a comma after it to begin the new sentence. Choices A, B, and D violate this rule with incorrect commas and colons. This matches choice C — C is correct.
